methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $1.0B - $1.5B.

data.narrative:
SkySafe, a Series B-stage defense and security company that has raised $50M+ to date, was valued using stage-progression and peer-median methodologies against a universe of six same-sector, same-stage private comparables supplemented by two public defense technology companies. The peer set spans Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns, with public references including DRSHF and IDEAFORGE.NS.

The stage-progression method applied a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $50M raised proxy, yielding $95M. The peer-median approach identified a $1.2B median valuation across the six-company comparable set, with a range spanning $1.0B to $1.5B. These two methods produced a wide spread reflecting the current stage and capital-raising posture of the target relative to more mature peer valuations in the defense tech subsector.

Triangulation of both methods implies a low bound of $81M, a median of $648M, and a high bound of $1.4B. The substantial dispersion—spanning over one order of magnitude—reflects material uncertainty around whether SkySafe's current traction and differentiation align with peer-median multiples or remain closer to stage-typical valuation levels. Key sensitivities include revenue trajectory, customer concentration in federal accounts, and competitive positioning relative to the named peer set.
